name: Wiggins
biography: Wiggins is the acknowledged leader of the Baker Street Irregulars, a rotating band of street children Holmes employs as a surveillance and intelligence network. Holmes prizes them because they can go anywhere without attracting attention — they see everything and hear everything, and a child loitering on a street corner draws no suspicion. In The Sign of the Four, Wiggins leads a dozen ragged urchins to 221B, delivers his report to Holmes with considerable self-possession, and collects three shillings a day plus a guinea for actionable intelligence. Watson notes that Wiggins enters the sitting room and stands at ease with the air of someone accustomed to the place. A similar unnamed leader commands the Irregulars in A Study in Scarlet; many readers take him to be the same boy. Wiggins represents Holmes's recognition that a detective's reach depends on people who move through strata he cannot enter without detection.
